steps.label: Project
steps.help: Creates the source/work/review/out/render layout and the project.yml that the later steps read defaults from. Always runs first.

steps.label: Safe Zone
steps.hint: Derive the safe-zone spec from a template PNG.
steps.help: Turns a design template (with the danger region marked) into the machine-readable safe-zone polygon that caption placement and QC use.

steps.label: Reframe
steps.hint: Reframe the source to the target format.
steps.help: Tracks the subject and crops the source to the target format — portrait, square, or landscape. Skippable when the source is already in-aspect.

steps.label: Rough Cut
steps.help: Transcribes the clip and proposes a tightened cut (drops filler, false starts, dead air) as an editable decision file you can hand-tune before rendering.

steps.label: Captions
steps.help: Builds glossary-corrected word-timed cues, lets you edit the caption file, and renders the styled caption overlay layer.

steps.label: Safe-Zone QC
steps.hint: Check captions/faces against the danger zone.
steps.help: Advisory check: flags protected elements intruding on the danger polygon and captions over the speaker's face. Advises, never blocks.

steps.label: Overlays
steps.hint: Timed/placed content overlays (image / video / source card).
steps.help: Places content overlays — a still, a video asset, or a generated article card — on a source-time window proposed from the transcript, as an editable overlay.def. Renders the placed/timed composite and emits overlay.occupancy so captions dodge overlays and QC stays aware.

steps.label: Composite
steps.hint: Flatten the layers into one preview render.
steps.help: Stacks the base and the caption/overlay layers into a single previewable video — the in-app preview of the assembled result and the guide track the editor handoffs carry on top. A review/handoff intermediate, not the final cut (that is yours, from your NLE).

steps.label: Output / Packaging
steps.hint: Package the project for your editor.
steps.help: Editor handoffs: enable the target(s) you want and they run in the batch like any other step. Premiere (FCP7 XML) and Final Cut / Resolve (FCPXML) assemble the cut + caption + composite-guide tracks; CapCut gathers the rendered layers into a folder (it imports no project file).

tasks.label: Initialize project

params.help: Assembled to the convention "YYYY-MM-DD <Token> Project - <Hook>" from the fields below, so it always matches.

parts.label: Token
parts.hint: Content-type token; part of the folder name and the render filename.

parts.label: Hook
parts.hint: A short description — becomes the render-file slug.

params.help: Pick the source clip. On run it is copied into the project — archived in source/ and seeded as the base layer (work/base.mp4) — so the reframe / rough-cut steps have their input. Browse or drag a file onto the field.
Selects the caption style + glossary set for this creator identity (e.g. a DH or SIGIL.ZERO identity).
The target frame. reels-9x16 (1080x1920) is the default for vertical Reels; the feed-* presets cover 4:5, 1:1, and 16:9. Drives crop math and safe-zone derivation.
Where the project folder is created. Defaults to ~/Video/Projects.

params.hint: Seed the project to preserve filler.
params.help: Sets the project default so rough cut keeps audio continuity (no filler/false-start trimming) unless overridden per run.

tasks.help: Creates the project layout and project.yml. The folder name encodes date/token/hook; identity and profile seed the project defaults.

tasks.label: Generate safe-zone spec

params.help: none = the full frame is safe; generic = per-aspect conservative inset rectangle (the default); custom = trace a template PNG for the exact destination. Generic social safe zones are conservative estimates. For platform-specific publishing, use a safe-zone template for the exact destination platform and placement.

params.hint: Template PNG with the danger region marked (custom mode).
params.help: The design template image (custom mode only). The marked region becomes the danger polygon (notch-aware).
The target frame. reels-9x16 (1080x1920) is the default for vertical Reels; the feed-* presets cover 4:5, 1:1, and 16:9. Drives crop math and safe-zone derivation.

params.hint: How the danger region is marked (custom mode).
params.help: auto detects alpha vs a key color; force alpha or color if detection guesses wrong.

tasks.hint: Derive the safe-zone spec (none / generic / custom).
tasks.help: Builds the safe-zone spec consumed downstream. INI-091 modes: none (full frame is safe), generic (per-aspect conservative insets, the default), or custom (trace a design template PNG where the danger region is marked by alpha or a key color).

tasks.label: Reframe to target format

params.help: The output shape. full-portrait (9:16) is the short-form default; also portrait (2:3), wide-portrait (4:5), square (1:1), widescreen (16:9), cinematic (21:9), classic-tv (4:3). Drives the crop geometry.
Auto picks the highest tier that fits the crop without upscaling beyond 5%; or force 4k / 1440p / 1080p / 720p. Separate from aspect.

performer = widest crop, face high, lower band kept for torso/props and captions; talking-head = zoomed on the face; wide-context = scene kept, subject centred. Leave unset for the legacy centred crop.
static picks one crop window for the whole clip (predictable); dynamic pans to follow the subject (more motion).
opencv is dependency-light; mediapipe is the higher-quality daily-driver face/pose tracker.

Overrides the framing intent's zoom. 1.0 = widest native crop (the most of the source the aspect allows, no fill); higher punches in. There is no pull-back past 1.0 by design — native is the widest. Leave unset to let Framing decide.
Where the subject sits vertically: -1 = top, 0 = centred, +1 = bottom. Only bites when there is vertical slack (punched in, or a source taller than the target). Unset = let Framing decide.
Where the crop window sits horizontally: 0 = hard left, 0.5 = centred, 1 = hard right. The set-box placement the composition lock holds on the X axis. Unset = let the tracker decide.
Where the crop window sits vertically: 0 = top, 0.5 = centred, 1 = bottom. The set-box placement the composition lock holds on the Y axis. Unset = let the tracker decide.
Hold the set-box on the given axis instead of following the subject: none = follow (default), x = hold horizontal, y = hold vertical, both = a fully static composed crop.
Computes and prints the crop plan but writes no video — fast way to sanity-check tracking before a full render.

tasks.hint: Crop the source to the target format (portrait, square, or landscape).
tasks.help: Subject-tracking crop. Static holds one crop; dynamic follows the subject. Daily-driver path needs MediaPipe; --dry-run plans without rendering.

tasks.label: Propose rough cut

params.help: Disables filler/dead-air trimming — one whole-clip KEEP, no speech edits. Use when continuity matters more than tightness.

params.hint: Dead-air gap (s) that triggers a cut.
params.help: Silence longer than this (seconds) is treated as dead air and removed. Lower = tighter, more aggressive.

params.hint: Lead padding before each kept segment (s).
params.help: Asymmetric cut padding. Small lead avoids clipping the first word's onset.

params.hint: Tail padding after each kept segment (s).
params.help: Larger tail than lead avoids clipping word-ends (a real defect found in Phase-2 testing).
mlx-whisper runs offline by default; --online permits a one-time model fetch.

tasks.hint: Transcribe + propose an editable cut decision file.
tasks.help: Produces the decision file (one scannable line per segment) you can hand-edit before rendering. Daily driver transcribes with mlx-whisper unless a --transcript is supplied.

tasks.label: Render rough cut

params.hint: Show the trim/concat plan without rendering.

tasks.hint: Re-render the cut from the (edited) decision file.
tasks.help: Applies the decision file's KEEP segments to the source via ffmpeg trim/concat. Re-runnable after you hand-edit the decision file.

tasks.label: Build caption file

params.help: The target frame. reels-9x16 (1080x1920) is the default for vertical Reels; the feed-* presets cover 4:5, 1:1, and 16:9. Drives crop math and safe-zone derivation.
Karaoke mode highlights the currently-spoken word within each cue.
The font the captions render in. Curated allowlist (the same set the pipeline accepts); Remotion falls back to Helvetica until font loading lands (INI-088 Phase 4).

params.hint: Caption font size (px at native height).
params.help: Pixel size at the profile's native height (reels = 1920px tall). Capped to a legible range at the Python boundary.
Hex color of the caption text fill, e.g. #FFFFFF. A color-picker widget is deferred; enter hex for v1.
Hex color of the caption text outline, e.g. #000000.
Outline thickness in px; 0 disables the stroke. Capped at the Python boundary.

params.hint: Draw a background plate behind the captions.
params.help: A whole-block rounded rectangle behind the caption text, padded to clear the stroke. Off by default.
Hex fill of the background plate, e.g. #000000.
Rounded-corner radius of the plate in px; 0 = square corners.

params.hint: Horizontal placement of the caption block.
params.help: clear-notch fills the widest notch-free span (wider, may bias left of frame-center at lower-third); center keeps the block symmetric about frame-center while still clearing the notch.
Lower bound of the words-per-cue range. 1/1 gives single-word cues; widen for phrase cues.
Upper bound of the words-per-cue range (e.g. 4 for 2/4 phrasing).

tasks.help: Produces the editable caption file (phrase-aware, balanced cues). --safezone lets it emit the styled props; you can hand-edit the caption file before rendering.

tasks.label: Render caption overlay

params.hint: Subject occupancy from reframe (optional) — captions dodge it.
params.help: Point at the reframe step's occupancy file (work/reframe.occupancy.json, INI-090 Phase 2). When set, captions relocate off the subject; absent or missing, captions render normally.
Forces the caption band; overrides the style default and any framing-intent hint carried in the occupancy file.
The font the captions render in. Curated allowlist (the same set the pipeline accepts); Remotion falls back to Helvetica until font loading lands (INI-088 Phase 4).

params.hint: Caption font size (px at native height).
params.help: Pixel size at the profile's native height (reels = 1920px tall). Capped to a legible range at the Python boundary.
Hex color of the caption text fill, e.g. #FFFFFF. A color-picker widget is deferred; enter hex for v1.
Hex color of the caption text outline, e.g. #000000.
Outline thickness in px; 0 disables the stroke. Capped at the Python boundary.

params.hint: Draw a background plate behind the captions.
params.help: A whole-block rounded rectangle behind the caption text, padded to clear the stroke. Off by default.
Hex fill of the background plate, e.g. #000000.
Rounded-corner radius of the plate in px; 0 = square corners.

params.hint: Horizontal placement of the caption block.
params.help: clear-notch fills the widest notch-free span (wider, may bias left of frame-center at lower-third); center keeps the block symmetric about frame-center while still clearing the notch.

tasks.hint: Render the styled caption overlay layer (alpha .mov).
tasks.help: Rebuilds props from the (possibly hand-edited) caption file + style/safe-zone config and renders the transparent caption overlay via Remotion (daily driver). This is the previewable caption layer the NLE stacks on top.

tasks.label: Build caption preview

params.help: The target frame. reels-9x16 (1080x1920) is the default for vertical Reels; the feed-* presets cover 4:5, 1:1, and 16:9. Drives crop math and safe-zone derivation.

tasks.hint: Checkerboard-baked preview of the caption layer.
tasks.help: Renders the transparent caption overlay over a neutral checkerboard into plain h264 so the previewer can play it in isolation. Optional — enable it only when you want to preview the caption layer in the app.

tasks.label: Safe-zone QC

params.help: Turns advisory warnings into a hard failure (exit 1) — for scripted gating, not the interactive default.

tasks.hint: Advisory: flag danger-zone intrusions + captions over faces.
tasks.help: Checks protected elements against the danger polygon (notch-aware) and captions over the speaker's face. Advisory only — it warns at the export/composite control but never blocks (SADD §4.2). --strict makes it exit non-zero for CI gating.

tasks.label: Build overlay file

params.help: The target frame. reels-9x16 (1080x1920) is the default for vertical Reels; the feed-* presets cover 4:5, 1:1, and 16:9. Drives crop math and safe-zone derivation.

params.hint: Word-level transcript JSON for window proposing.
params.help: When given, an overlay row's window can be proposed from the span where its subject is discussed (fill the row's 'at' field with a phrase). The AI-leverage step; the LLM never touches the render path.

params.hint: Base clip name recorded in the file header (advisory).
params.help: A label written into the file's `source:` header for reference. Not the asset and not the base video — the base is supplied at render time (Render overlays → base video).

params.hint: The overlays to place — one row each.
params.help: Each row is one overlay. Pick a Kind, point it at an asset path (Src), set its on-screen window (Start/End seconds, or a Discussed phrase with a Transcript loaded), and choose a Placement (full-bleed / bottom-half / a PiP rect). A video can duck or mute its own audio. Each row emits one --add entry.

row.label: Kind
row.hint: image = still; video = clip; card = generated source card.

row.label: Src (asset path)
row.hint: Path to the image/video asset (or the rendered card).

row.label: Discussed phrase
row.hint: Propose the window from where this is discussed.

row.label: Placement
row.hint: Where it sits; pip-rect needs a Rect.

row.label: Transition
row.hint: Hard cut or a fade in/out.

row.label: Label
row.hint: A human label recorded with the overlay.

tasks.hint: Add one or more overlays — image, video, or source card.
tasks.help: Writes overlay.def — one line per overlay (kind, src, window, placement, transition). Add overlays in the Overlays table below; each row becomes a --add entry. Set a window explicitly (start/end) or let it be proposed from the spoken span (a phrase in 'at' + a Transcript). You can also hand-edit the file afterward, then render.

tasks.label: Capture source card
tasks.hint: Capture an article/page into editable card content.
tasks.help: Fetches a URL (Chrome / Jina, daily-driver) and structures it into a reviewable card.content JSON (heading/body/footer/image/citation) you edit before rendering the card overlay.

tasks.label: Render overlays

params.hint: Show the ffmpeg command without rendering.

tasks.hint: Composite the placed/timed overlays + emit occupancy.
tasks.help: Reads the (edited) overlay.def, places/scales/times each overlay over the base, and writes the preview composite + the overlay.occupancy descriptor (frame size from the safe-zone spec). Re-runnable after editing overlay.def.

tasks.label: Composite layers

params.help: Quality of the composite render; 18 is near-visually-lossless. This is a preview/handoff render, not your final master.

params.hint: Show the ffmpeg command without rendering.

tasks.hint: Flatten base + overlay layers into review/composite.mp4.
tasks.help: Stacks the layers bottom-to-top by z-order (base, then the caption overlay + any future overlays) into one previewable .mp4 — the in-app preview of the assembled result and the guide track the editor handoffs carry on top. QC advises but never blocks it. A review/handoff intermediate, not your final cut.

tasks.label: Export — Premiere Pro
tasks.hint: FCP7 XML (XMEML) — opens in Premiere Pro.
tasks.help: Assembles the base cut (the decision's KEEP segments over the reframed-uncut clip, as trimmable clips) + caption track + the composite as a disabled top guide track, as FCP7/XMEML XML. Premiere does not import FCPXML, so this is its dedicated target.

tasks.label: Export — Final Cut / Resolve
tasks.hint: FCPXML 1.10 — Final Cut Pro / DaVinci Resolve.
tasks.help: The same timeline (base cut + captions + composite guide) serialized as FCPXML 1.10 for Final Cut Pro and DaVinci Resolve.

tasks.label: Export — CapCut
tasks.help: Gathers the rendered layers (the cut base + caption overlay) and the composite into a folder with a README listing the z-order, for hand assembly in CapCut.

artifacts.help: Produced by project-init; consumed as an ordering dependency by steps that write into the project but don't read a media channel (e.g. safe-zone generation).
The base video channel. project.init seeds it; reframe and roughcut.render each rewrite it. The previewer's base layer.

A stable copy of the reframed-but-uncut video, written by reframe alongside base.mp4. The editor handoffs reference this (not base, which roughcut.render rewrites with the cut) so the decision file's KEEP segments lay over the full clip as separate, trimmable clips.

artifacts.hint: The subject's footprint in the reframed frame.
artifacts.help: Written by reframe (INI-090): the tracked subject's box projected into the target frame, as caption avoid-windows. The caption render optionally consumes it so captions dodge the subject.
Machine-readable danger/safe polygon (notch-aware). A descriptor: caption placement and QC read it; no branch reads another's pixels.
The product of the rough-cut step — one scannable KEEP/DROP line per segment. Hand-edit it, then roughcut.render applies it.
Word-timed, glossary-corrected cues. Hand-edit before rendering.
The rendered caption layer the NLE stacks on top. Transparent HEVC-alpha — not previewed directly (the webview can't be relied on to decode alpha); the caption.preview proxy is its previewable form.
The caption overlay baked over a neutral checkerboard into plain h264 so the previewer can play it in isolation. A GUI-only proxy — never bundled into an editor export.
One line per overlay (kind, src, source-time window, placement, transition). The product — hand-edit before rendering, mirrors roughcut.def / caption.def.
heading / body / footer / image / citation captured from a URL. Reviewable JSON the card overlay renders from.
The rect + time window each overlay occupies. A descriptor: caption placement dodges it and QC flags intrusions; no branch reads another's pixels.
The base with the overlay layers placed/timed/faded into one previewable .mp4. A review intermediate, not the final cut.
JSON report of danger-zone intrusions and caption-over-face hits. Advisory input to the export/composite warning.

The base + caption/overlay layers composited into one .mp4 — the in-app 'assembled result' preview and the muted guide track the editor handoffs include on top (highest z-order). A preview/handoff intermediate in review/, not the final cut (that is render/, yours from the NLE).
The assembled editor project (FCP7/XMEML XML) the Premiere export writes; open it in Premiere Pro.
The assembled editor project (FCPXML 1.10) for Final Cut Pro / DaVinci Resolve.
The folder of rendered layers + composite + README the CapCut export gathers for hand assembly.
